summary: Verified ADR 0157 live on the production Windmill worker checkout after converging current main onto docker-runtime-lv3, confirming the execution-lane catalog and scheduler lane module were deployed byte-for-byte, and validating the live lane catalog in place.

verification.observed: Repeated `make live-apply-service service=windmill env=production` runs from current main deployed the worker checkout and converged proxmox-host plus postgres-lv3, but both recaps ended with a late docker-runtime-lv3 SSH disconnect during the Windmill reseed path after the runtime had already been updated.

Direct probes on docker-runtime-lv3 confirmed `/srv/platform_server/config/execution-lanes.yaml`, `/srv/platform_server/platform/scheduler/lanes.py`, and `/srv/platform_server/scripts/execution_lanes.py` matched the current main checksums `8d4e2da0...`, `af08231c...`, and `1e2c2490...`.

A direct guest-local run of `python3 /srv/platform_server/scripts/execution_lanes.py --validate` on docker-runtime-lv3 returned `Execution lanes OK: /srv/platform_server/config/execution-lanes.yaml (9 lane(s))`.
A direct guest-local probe on docker-runtime-lv3 returned `CE v1.662.0` from `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/version` on 2026-03-25.

notes: Earlier on 2026-03-25 the same live apply was blocked before guest convergence because proxmox-host was unreachable on the Tailscale SSH path; that transport issue was resolved before the successful deployment verification recorded here.

The current Windmill seed catalog still contains two repo-managed entries for `f/lv3/scheduler_watchdog_loop`, which produces a path-conflict response during manual create replay and likely contributes noise during the late reseed step even though the ADR 0157 deployment itself is present and validated.
